issues.message: V0-V1 move in parallel Major 3rd from beat 10.5 to 12 (2 transitions, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.

V0-V1 travel in parallel Perfect 4th from beat 19 to 20 (1 transition, strength 1.4 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V2 travel in parallel Minor 6th (+1 oct) from beat 18.5 to 20 (2 transitions, strength 1.8 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V1-V2 move in parallel Unison (+1 oct) from beat 43.5 to 44 (1 transition, strength 10.0 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.

V0-V1 travel in parallel Perfect 4th from beat 47 to 48 (1 transition, strength 1.4 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V2 travel in parallel Minor 6th (+1 oct) from beat 46.5 to 48 (2 transitions, strength 1.8 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V2 move in parallel Major 3rd (+1 oct) from beat 61 to 62 (1 transition, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.
Adjacent voices V1-V2 are 21 semitones apart at beat 30.

Voice pairs share one attack clock for 57% of their time (V0-V1 0.68, V0-V2 0.67, V1-V2 0.37): counter-lines are harmonizing rather than moving in independent rhythmic strata. Deliberate in homophony/chorale textures; in counterpoint, put the countersubject at a different rate (double-speed cells or augmentation) attacking in the other voice's gaps.
